Stefan Marinovic.bio: Experienced New Zealand goalkeeper from Wellington Phoenix in the A-League. With 30 caps, he has been a long-standing member of the All Whites and brings domestic league leadership.
Stefan Marinovic.playingStyle: Tall, commanding goalkeeper who dominates his area and provides solid shot-stopping and distribution
Stefan Marinovic.worldCup2026Role: Starting goalkeeper or strong competition for the number one jersey
Stefan Marinovic.notableFact: A Wellington Phoenix stalwart who has been a key figure in the club's A-League campaigns

Bill Tuiloma.bio: Versatile center-back with experience in South American football at Huracan in Argentina's top division. With 40 caps and 3 goals, he is one of the most experienced defenders in the squad.
Bill Tuiloma.playingStyle: Mobile, athletic center-back who can also play as a defensive midfielder and is comfortable with the ball at his feet
Bill Tuiloma.worldCup2026Role: Key center-back whose experience in competitive South American football is invaluable
Bill Tuiloma.notableFact: Born in New Zealand with Tongan heritage, previously played for the Portland Timbers in MLS before moving to Argentina

Nando Pijnaker.bio: Dutch-born center-back who represents New Zealand through family heritage, playing for Portland Timbers in MLS. A tall, physical defender with 20 caps for the All Whites.
Nando Pijnaker.playingStyle: Imposing, physical center-back who excels in aerial duels and uses his height to dominate opposing strikers
Nando Pijnaker.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back providing height and physical presence in the defensive unit
Nando Pijnaker.notableFact: Born in the Netherlands, he chose to represent New Zealand through his Kiwi family connections

Michael Boxall.bio: Veteran defender who plays for Minnesota United in MLS and is the most experienced center-back in the squad with 45 caps. At 37, he brings invaluable leadership and calm to the backline.
Michael Boxall.playingStyle: Experienced, steady defender who reads the game exceptionally well and organizes those around him
Michael Boxall.worldCup2026Role: Veteran defensive leader whose experience and composure are vital for managing World Cup pressure
Michael Boxall.notableFact: Would be one of the oldest players at the 2026 World Cup, bringing wisdom from a long international career

Tim Payne.bio: Wellington Phoenix left-back who provides a reliable option on the left side of defense. A natural left-footer with 15 caps for New Zealand.
Tim Payne.playingStyle: Solid, dependable left-back who focuses on defensive duties while providing width when the team attacks
Tim Payne.worldCup2026Role: Backup left-back providing squad depth and a natural left-footed option
Tim Payne.notableFact: Has been a consistent performer for Wellington Phoenix in the A-League for several seasons

Joe Bell.bio: Central midfielder playing in the Belgian First Division with Standard Liege. One of New Zealand's most talented midfielders with 25 caps and 2 goals, he has carved out a career in European football.
Joe Bell.playingStyle: Technical, composed midfielder with excellent passing range and the ability to control the tempo of matches
Joe Bell.worldCup2026Role: Key central midfielder who dictates the pace of play and connects defense to attack
Joe Bell.notableFact: Moved to Europe via the Portland Timbers in MLS, demonstrating New Zealand's pathway to competitive football

Elijah Just.bio: Wellington Phoenix right winger who provides pace and directness on the flank. With 15 caps and 2 goals, he has become a regular in the All Whites setup.
Elijah Just.playingStyle: Direct, pacy winger who takes on defenders and delivers crosses into dangerous areas
Elijah Just.worldCup2026Role: Starting or rotation option on the right wing, providing width and attacking threat
Elijah Just.notableFact: A homegrown Wellington Phoenix product who has developed into a full international

Alex Greive.bio: Young striker playing for the New York Red Bulls in MLS. With 10 caps and 3 goals, he represents the next generation of New Zealand attacking talent and has shown a keen eye for goal.
Alex Greive.playingStyle: Energetic, mobile striker who presses from the front and has good finishing instincts
Alex Greive.worldCup2026Role: Forward option providing energy, pressing, and goal threat as a backup or partner to Chris Wood
Alex Greive.notableFact: Made the move from A-League football to MLS, continuing New Zealand's tradition of developing players in Australasian and American leagues

Chris Wood.bio: New Zealand's all-time star player and captain, playing for Nottingham Forest in the Premier League. With 35 goals in 75 caps, he is the undisputed leader and talisman of the All Whites.
Chris Wood.playingStyle: Classic target man with excellent aerial ability, clinical finishing, and the ability to hold the ball up for teammates
Chris Wood.worldCup2026Role: Captain, starting striker, and the player around whom New Zealand's entire attacking strategy revolves
Chris Wood.notableFact: Has scored consistently in the Premier League across multiple clubs, making him by far the highest-profile New Zealand footballer of his generation

Marko Stamenic.bio: Talented young midfielder at Red Bull Salzburg in the Austrian Bundesliga. At 25, he is one of the most promising players in the squad with 20 caps and 2 goals.
Marko Stamenic.playingStyle: Dynamic, box-to-box midfielder who covers ground quickly, carries the ball well, and contributes in both attack and defense
Marko Stamenic.worldCup2026Role: Starting central midfielder providing dynamism and European-quality play in the engine room
Marko Stamenic.notableFact: His move to Red Bull Salzburg, a consistent Champions League club, represents one of the biggest transfers in New Zealand football history

Kosta Barbarouses.bio: Veteran winger of Greek heritage with 50 caps and 9 goals for New Zealand. The Wellington Phoenix attacker has been a loyal servant of the national team for over a decade.
Kosta Barbarouses.playingStyle: Experienced, intelligent winger who uses guile and positioning rather than pure pace to create and score goals
Kosta Barbarouses.worldCup2026Role: Experienced attacking option who brings calm and composure in big moments
Kosta Barbarouses.notableFact: Has represented New Zealand for over a decade and is one of the most capped active All Whites players

Max Crocombe.bio: Goalkeeper playing for Oxford United in the English Championship. With 10 caps, he provides experienced backup and competition in the goalkeeping department.
Max Crocombe.playingStyle: Agile, shot-stopping goalkeeper with quick reflexes and the ability to make crucial saves in tight situations
Max Crocombe.worldCup2026Role: Second-choice goalkeeper providing quality backup and competitive pressure for the starting spot
Max Crocombe.notableFact: His experience in the English Championship gives New Zealand a goalkeeper familiar with high-intensity, physical football

Storm Roux.bio: Right-back from Melbourne City in the A-League with 25 caps and 1 goal. A steady, reliable defender who has been a consistent presence in the All Whites setup.
Storm Roux.playingStyle: Reliable right-back who balances defensive duties with overlapping runs and provides consistent performances
Storm Roux.worldCup2026Role: Starting or backup right-back providing defensive reliability and experience
Storm Roux.notableFact: Has been one of the most consistent performers in the A-League across multiple seasons

Ryan Thomas.bio: Experienced midfielder at PSV Eindhoven in the Dutch Eredivisie. With 25 caps and 2 goals, he is one of the highest-profile New Zealand players in European football.
Ryan Thomas.playingStyle: Versatile, hard-working midfielder who can play multiple positions and contributes defensive solidity and passing quality
Ryan Thomas.worldCup2026Role: Key midfield option whose Dutch league experience and versatility make him invaluable to the squad
Ryan Thomas.notableFact: Has spent most of his career in the Netherlands, becoming a trusted squad player at one of the biggest clubs in Dutch football

Clayton Lewis.bio: Wellington Phoenix midfielder who provides domestic league depth in the All Whites midfield. With 20 caps, he is a reliable squad member who knows the national team system well.
Clayton Lewis.playingStyle: Industrious, hard-working midfielder who keeps things simple and provides energy and defensive cover
Clayton Lewis.worldCup2026Role: Squad depth midfielder who can step in to provide energy and defensive cover when needed
Clayton Lewis.notableFact: A Wellington Phoenix academy product who has become a regular feature in the national team

Liberato Cacace.bio: Dynamic left-back at Empoli in Serie A, making him one of the most high-profile New Zealand players in European football. At 25, his 30 caps and Italian league experience are invaluable.
Liberato Cacace.playingStyle: Attack-minded left-back with excellent technical ability, dribbling skills, and the confidence to get forward and create chances
Liberato Cacace.worldCup2026Role: Starting left-back and one of the team's most dangerous attacking outlets from defensive positions
Liberato Cacace.notableFact: One of the highest-valued New Zealand players, his Serie A experience gives the All Whites genuine quality at full-back

Sarpreet Singh.bio: Creative midfielder of Indian-Kiwi heritage playing in Germany's second tier with Regensburg. A former Bayern Munich youth player with 15 caps and 1 goal, he brings unique flair to the squad.
Sarpreet Singh.playingStyle: Creative, left-footed playmaker with excellent vision, technical ability, and the skill to play between the lines
Sarpreet Singh.worldCup2026Role: Creative attacking option who can unlock defenses with his passing and movement in the final third
Sarpreet Singh.notableFact: Was the first New Zealander to sign for Bayern Munich, spending time in their reserve team before moving on

Dane Ingham.bio: Experienced right-back from Brisbane Roar in the A-League with 20 caps for New Zealand. A reliable, versatile defender who provides depth across the back four.
Dane Ingham.playingStyle: Steady, consistent full-back who prioritizes defensive duties and provides reliable width when called upon
Dane Ingham.worldCup2026Role: Backup full-back providing squad depth across the defensive positions
Dane Ingham.notableFact: Has been a consistent performer across multiple A-League clubs throughout his career

Matthew Garbett.bio: Exciting young midfielder on Napoli's books in Serie A, one of the most promising talents in New Zealand football history. At just 24, his 10 caps and Italian football education mark him as a future star.
Matthew Garbett.playingStyle: Dynamic, energetic midfielder with excellent technical ability, sharp passing, and the drive to make things happen
Matthew Garbett.worldCup2026Role: Emerging midfield talent who can inject quality and energy from the bench or start depending on form
Matthew Garbett.notableFact: Being at Napoli puts him at one of Europe's most prestigious clubs, making him one of the highest-profile All Whites players ever

Andre De Jong.bio: Veteran center-back playing in the USL Championship with Phoenix Rising. At 36 with 10 caps, he provides experienced defensive depth and mentorship for younger players.
Andre De Jong.playingStyle: Experienced, physical defender who uses his knowledge and positioning to compensate for pace at the highest level
Andre De Jong.worldCup2026Role: Veteran squad player providing defensive emergency cover and dressing room experience
Andre De Jong.notableFact: One of the oldest members of the All Whites squad, bringing years of professional experience across multiple leagues

Oskar van Hattum.bio: Young goalkeeper from Wellington Phoenix who represents the future of New Zealand goalkeeping. At 24 with 5 caps, his inclusion in the squad demonstrates investment in young talent.
Oskar van Hattum.playingStyle: Tall, athletic young goalkeeper with modern distribution skills and shot-stopping ability
Oskar van Hattum.worldCup2026Role: Third-choice goalkeeper gaining invaluable World Cup experience for the future
Oskar van Hattum.notableFact: Part of the Wellington Phoenix academy production line that has been central to developing New Zealand international players

Logan Rogerson.bio: Young right winger from Wellington Phoenix who is one of the most promising attackers in New Zealand football. At 22 with 5 caps, he brings youthful energy and excitement to the squad.
Logan Rogerson.playingStyle: Quick, direct young winger with good dribbling ability and the confidence to take on defenders
Logan Rogerson.worldCup2026Role: Young attacking option who can provide a spark off the bench with fresh legs and fearlessness
Logan Rogerson.notableFact: One of several Wellington Phoenix products in the squad, showing the club's vital role in developing All Whites talent

Gianni Stensness.bio: Australian-born center-back of Norwegian-Kiwi heritage who plays for Viking in Norway's Eliteserien. With 15 caps, he adds versatility and international defensive experience to the squad.
Gianni Stensness.playingStyle: Composed, technical defender who reads the game well and is comfortable playing out from the back under pressure
Gianni Stensness.worldCup2026Role: Versatile defensive option who can play center-back or defensive midfield as needed
Gianni Stensness.notableFact: Born in Australia with Norwegian and New Zealand heritage, he chose to represent New Zealand at international level

Ben Waine.bio: Young striker at Plymouth Argyle in the English Championship, one of the bright young talents in New Zealand football. With 15 caps and 4 goals, he has an impressive international scoring rate.
Ben Waine.playingStyle: Sharp, mobile striker with good finishing instincts, intelligent movement, and improving all-round play
Ben Waine.worldCup2026Role: Key backup striker to Chris Wood, capable of starting or making an impact off the bench
Ben Waine.notableFact: His goalscoring form in the English Championship has made him one of the most exciting New Zealand strikers of the new generation

Jesse Randall.bio: Young left winger at Vitesse in the Dutch Eredivisie, one of the emerging talents in New Zealand football. At 23 with 5 caps and 1 goal, his European experience adds quality to the wide positions.
Jesse Randall.playingStyle: Pacy, direct left winger with good dribbling skills, a dangerous left foot, and the ability to cut inside and shoot
Jesse Randall.worldCup2026Role: Young wide attacking option bringing European quality and pace from the left flank
Jesse Randall.notableFact: One of a growing number of New Zealand players making their way into European football through the Dutch league pathway

Jay Herdman.bio: Young midfielder at Sheffield United in the English Championship, the youngest member of New Zealand's squad. With 5 caps, his inclusion reflects his exciting potential and maturity beyond his years.
Jay Herdman.playingStyle: Energetic, technically sound young midfielder who plays with maturity and composure beyond his age
Jay Herdman.worldCup2026Role: Young developmental talent experiencing the World Cup environment for future benefit to New Zealand football
Jay Herdman.notableFact: Son of New Zealand head coach John Herdman, bringing a unique family connection to the national team setup
